message: Jake
Yuriko
Ash
Sorry to keep you waiting. The boss is messing around with some strange guy.
Maybe we could have gotten some important information.
With that attitude, it seems you tangled with a homeless person. Luckily, this key works, so let's get inside.
Did you try the key?
Yeah. It would be a big deal if it were a trap from the key's owner.
That's true.
Ah, what a difference from a fool like the boss.
I'm sorry for being a fool.
Anyway.
Oh, look, we've arrived. This is the room in question.
I don't know...
This room is surprisingly small.
But wow, the smell is really strong.
It feels like the smell will cling to my clothes.
Yeah.
Staying here too long might give me a headache. I thought this room would feel a bit larger.
There's a door, but unfortunately, it's locked.
I wonder if there's something in there?
Still, staying here too long might give me a headache. Can't we do something about this smell?
Is there something behind that locked door causing this strong scent?
Sniff sniff.
Not perfume, but room cologne? But it’s super intense!
Should we hurry and check the room? But is it really okay to do this?
What are you talking about? If you’re acting all innocent while the key is right in front of you, it clearly means you have something to hide.
Right? The boss really doesn't get it at all. Well then, let's investigate!
Investigate
System
Please left-click on the spot you want to investigate.
Right-click to return to command selection.
Save
Exit the game
Please left-click on the place you want to investigate.
Right-click to return to command selection.
It's a beautifully patterned sofa. Wasn't this made by some famous brand?
No, this isn't the time to be impressed.
At first glance, there are no stains or anything particularly concerning.
Since there's no dust, this sofa might have been used.
It doesn't look like there's anything wrong with the sofa itself.
This room doesn’t really give off the vibe of someone living here. The surface of the mirror is faintly covered in dust.
Normally, mirrors should be kept polished. Is the owner of this room someone who doesn't want to look in the mirror?
The fact that it’s this dusty probably means it doesn’t lead to a hidden door.
The bookshelf is lined with the complete works of famous authors, but have these books ever actually been read?
Boss, please don't interrupt. I'm currently looking for some clues.
A clue among the bookshelves?
There could be a code wedged between the books, or a key hidden by hollowing out the book.
Here,
she is carefully investigating. It's probably best to leave it to her.
There's a carpet laid on the wooden floor.
It seems there aren't any stains that I can see,
but with such a dark color, you wouldn't find stains unless you look very closely.
Interestingly, this carpet looks clean as if it has been recently cleaned. There's not a single piece of garbage lying around.
This must have been bought recently, or it’s likely been taken for cleaning.
Cushions of the same size and cover are lined up on the sofa.
Given the feel of the fabric, they seem to be relatively new.
When I picked one up and smelled it, the scent of the room was absorbed into it.
Then, it seems like it’s not exactly new.
I can't find anything particularly unusual.
A thin layer of dust has settled on the lampshade. Other than a strange smell, there's no sense of human life.
On closer inspection, this lamp's bulb is also quite dirty. Has it not been used for a long time?
This room is odd. It contains a mix of unused and brand new items.
On the fireplace, an empty photo frame, incense, and a small bottle are lined up.
Is the source of the scent drifting throughout the room this incense?
I smelled it, but it's a faint scent.
It seems that this scent is mingling with another strong smell, creating a strange odor.
The incense on the fireplace is just a long stick inserted into a stand that burns it without lighting it.
Other than the incense, there's dust piled up, making me wonder if the resident of this room is averse to cleaning.
The fireplace itself seems to be unused.
I tried to peek into the gap between the sofa and the carpet, but it was impossible, so I reached my hand in to search.
For now, no dust or trash stuck to my hand.
It seems like there’s nothing else.
I conducted another search by touch.
While I was stroking the nice carpet, I felt something with my hand. What I pulled out was a bear-shaped brooch.
What is that?
It seems to be a brooch, but...
It’s not the key to a locked door.
For now, I stuffed the brooch into my pocket.
The carpet is suspiciously clean.
I had a feeling there might be something here.
But so far, there's nothing.
I see.
It would be nice if there was a key for the door that won't open in this room.
This room is strange. Some things look like they haven't been used in ages, while others seem oddly new.
I’m starting to get a headache.
I usually think there’s something in places like this.
Isn't that just from reading too many detective stories?
Not at all!
Uh.
Hmm, it’s strange that there’s nothing.
Oh! Found it! Boss, I found the key! This has to be the key to the door that won't open.
Look, it’s exactly the same design as this door.
Where on earth was this?
There was a book on the shelf, hollowed out in the shape of a key, and it was neatly tucked inside.
I hope this really is the key to the door that won’t open.
What if it turns out to be a duplicate key to the door?
Wouldn't that be too much of a hassle to hide it in a book?
I’m a little curious, though.
Can I sprinkle some luminol?
Did you bring it?
Yeah, just to be safe.
Saying that,
(he) drew the curtains and began to sprinkle luminol in the areas that caught his attention.
After a while,
some reactions appeared in the room as if to prove that a murder had taken place here.
This is blood, right?
Wow.
Did a murder happen here? But whether it’s related to the series of incidents is...
Y-yeah, that's true.
I don't understand, right?
For a while, I stared blankly at the room.
Should we try the key together?
Y-yes! That’s right, isn’t it?
We can't forget, can we?
The key we found fit perfectly into the unyielding door's keyhole.
After all, it was the key for this place.
But I have a bad feeling.
I feel like I might regret going inside.
That's true. I hesitate to go in somehow.
O-please don't scare me like that!
W-Well then, I'll open the door, okay?
With a click, the key turned, and now, the unyielding door has opened.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
Ｖ＝
Ｎ＝
